themes.exampleQuote: Sahil has been very kind and helpful with accepting custom orders whenever I come with my friends.
Delicious food! Authentic flavors! Must visit place.
The staff were extremely rude and dismissive, which made the atmosphere feel unwelcoming.
Sahil has been very kind and helpful with accepting custom orders.
The cashier/manager failed to hide his arrogance and said it was the policy of the restaurant.

notable_quote: Reva first opened in June 2024 and I have been a regular customer ever since; the food taste is the best you can find in Pittsburgh and no other Indian restaurant can match the level.
sentiment_summary: Reviewers rally around the food quality and a standout server named Sahil, but a specific and recurring service inconsistency, staff who swing from gracious to dismissive depending on who is working, surfaces just enough to keep sentiment below where the food alone would put it.
quarter_narrative: Thirty-three Pittsburgh creators posted TikToks at Reva this quarter, logging 373,000-plus plays without a single post from the restaurant itself in the last 72 days. The most telling video came from @gabs_joella, who filmed a family visit to her mother's restaurant and pulled 66,300 plays on a caption about helping promote the family business. Reva holds the top spot in Pittsburgh Indian restaurants and sits fourth in the broader Asian Kitchens family, where Mola leads this issue on the strength of its own creator collaboration. The gap between Reva's creator footprint and its own silence on social is the clearest story here; closing it is also the most direct path toward that family lead.
tldr_read: Thirty-three creators filming, zero posts in 72 days, and the city's strongest Indian restaurant signal. Ones to Watch, #1 in Pittsburgh Indian restaurants.
tldr_meaning: The review volume and creator coverage are already doing heavy lifting; the Instagram signal going dark is the one variable holding Reva at fourth in the family instead of threatening Mola at the top.

diagnosis_pullquote.line: Number One In Indian, Carried By Creators. The Restaurant Has Gone Quiet.

playbook.headline: Restart Instagram before the signal fades
playbook.action: The account has been dark for 72 days with a 37.85% engagement rate sitting idle; one post resets the momentum clock.

playbook.headline: Claim the creator wave with reposts
playbook.action: Thirty-three creators have posted 373,326 plays this quarter; reposting even five of those videos costs nothing and fills the feed immediately.

playbook.headline: Address the service inconsistency publicly
playbook.action: Two negative reviews cite specific staff rudeness; a visible owner response pattern on Google lifts sentiment scores faster than new five-stars alone.
